name: fd
description: A simple, fast and user-friendly alternative to 'find'. While it does not aim to support all of find's powerful functionality, it provides sensible (opinionated) defaults for a majority of use cases with a colorized output and smart case by default.

globalOptions.name: --hidden
globalOptions.description: Include hidden directories and files in the search results. By default, hidden entries are skipped

globalOptions.name: --no-ignore
globalOptions.description: Show results that would otherwise be ignored by .gitignore, .ignore, .fdignore, or global ignore files

globalOptions.name: --no-ignore-vcs
globalOptions.description: Show results that would otherwise be ignored by .gitignore files only. Other ignore files are still respected

globalOptions.name: --no-ignore-parent
globalOptions.description: Do not respect ignore files from parent directories

globalOptions.name: --unrestricted
globalOptions.description: Perform an unrestricted search. Alias for --no-ignore --hidden. Use -uu to also show results from ignored and hidden files

globalOptions.name: --case-sensitive
globalOptions.description: Perform a case-sensitive search. By default, fd uses smart case (case-insensitive unless the pattern contains uppercase)

globalOptions.name: --ignore-case
globalOptions.description: Perform a case-insensitive search. By default, fd uses smart case matching

globalOptions.name: --glob
globalOptions.description: Perform a glob-based search instead of a regular expression search. If used with the -p/--full-path option, globs can match full paths with ** as separator

globalOptions.name: --regex
globalOptions.description: Perform a regular-expression based search (the default). This overrides --glob if both are set

globalOptions.name: --fixed-strings
globalOptions.description: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Note: using --glob with this option has no effect

globalOptions.name: --and
globalOptions.description: Add additional required search patterns. All --and patterns must match for a result to be returned

globalOptions.name: --type
globalOptions.description: Filter results by type: f (file), d (directory), l (symlink), x (executable), e (empty), s (socket), p (pipe), b (block-device), c (char-device). Can be specified multiple times

globalOptions.name: --extension
globalOptions.description: Filter results by file extension. Can be specified multiple times (e.g. -e js -e ts)

globalOptions.name: --exact-depth
globalOptions.description: Only show search results at the exact given depth relative to the search path

globalOptions.name: --min-depth
globalOptions.description: Only show search results at or below the given minimum depth relative to the search path

globalOptions.name: --max-depth
globalOptions.description: Limit the directory traversal to the given depth. Setting this to 1 only searches the immediate directory

globalOptions.name: --prune
globalOptions.description: Do not traverse into directories that match the search pattern. If matching a directory, none of its children will be examined

globalOptions.name: --exclude
globalOptions.description: Exclude entries matching the given glob pattern. This overrides any other ignore logic. Can be specified multiple times

globalOptions.name: --ignore-file
globalOptions.description: Add a custom ignore file with patterns to exclude from search results. This can be specified multiple times

globalOptions.name: --color
globalOptions.description: Control when to use colored output. Values: never, auto (default), always

globalOptions.name: --threads
globalOptions.description: Set the number of threads to use for searching and executing commands. Default: number of available CPU cores

globalOptions.name: --size
globalOptions.description: Limit results based on file size. Accepts suffixes: b, k, m, g, t, ki, mi, gi, ti. Prefix with + for minimum, - for maximum (e.g. '+1M', '-500k')

globalOptions.name: --changed-within
globalOptions.description: Filter results to only include entries that were changed within the given time. Accepts durations (10h, 1d, 35min) or dates (2018-10-27)

globalOptions.name: --changed-before
globalOptions.description: Filter results to only include entries that were changed before the given time. Same format as --changed-within

globalOptions.name: --owner
globalOptions.description: Filter by owning user and/or group. Format: user:group, user, :group, user: . On Windows, only user filtering is supported

globalOptions.name: --list-details
globalOptions.description: Use a long listing format with file metadata (permissions, owner, file size, modification time)

globalOptions.name: --absolute-path
globalOptions.description: Show absolute instead of relative paths. This can be useful when using fd in combination with other commands

globalOptions.name: --follow
globalOptions.description: Follow symbolic links. By default, fd does not follow symbolic links

globalOptions.name: --full-path
globalOptions.description: Match the full absolute path against the pattern rather than just the file name

globalOptions.name: --print0
globalOptions.description: Separate search results by the null character instead of newlines. Useful for piping results to xargs -0

globalOptions.name: --max-results
globalOptions.description: Limit the number of search results and quit once that number is reached

globalOptions.name: --one-file-system
globalOptions.description: Do not cross file system boundaries when traversing the directory tree

globalOptions.name: --exec
globalOptions.description: Execute a command for each search result in parallel. The following placeholders are available: {} (path), {/} (basename), {//} (parent), {.} (no ext), {/.} (basename no ext)

globalOptions.name: --exec-batch
globalOptions.description: Execute a command once with all search results as arguments. Same placeholders as --exec

globalOptions.name: --batch-size
globalOptions.description: Maximum number of arguments to pass to the command given with -X. If more than batch-size results, the command is run multiple times

globalOptions.name: --strip-cwd-prefix
globalOptions.description: Remove the './' prefix from paths when no search path is given and the output goes to a non-interactive terminal

globalOptions.name: --base-directory
globalOptions.description: Change the current directory to the given path before searching. Reported paths are always relative to the given directory

globalOptions.name: --path-separator
globalOptions.description: Set the path separator to use when printing file paths. The default is the OS-specific separator

globalOptions.name: --search-path
globalOptions.description: Specify a search path to use instead of the current directory. Can be specified multiple times

globalOptions.name: --show-errors
globalOptions.description: Enable the display of filesystem errors encountered during searching (e.g. permission denied)

globalOptions.name: --quiet
globalOptions.description: Print nothing, exit with code 0 if a match is found, 1 otherwise. Useful in shell scripts

globalOptions.name: --max-buffer-time
globalOptions.description: Amount of time in milliseconds to buffer, before streaming search results to the console

globalOptions.name: --gen-completions
globalOptions.description: Generate shell completions for the given shell (bash, zsh, fish, powershell, elvish)
